records.m_Talk_ENG: Having decided that, you called them to your office. They said they'd be here soon.

records.m_Talk_ENG: We remember. The days when I was just happy to smell the grass and feel the sun and cool breeze on my skin.
records.m_Talk_DEU: Wir erinnern uns. An die Tage, an denen wir einfach nur gl9588cklich waren, das Gras zu riechen und die Sonne und die k9588hle Brise auf unserer Haut zu sp9588ren.
records.m_Talk_FRA: Il y en a eu, des jours heureux. Le soleil irradiant la pleine, l'herbe glissant au bout des doigts, la brise fra95|che caressant la peau.

records.m_Talk_ENG: But when people close to us started dying from disease or accidents, we started having doubts.

records.m_Talk_ENG: Where does man come from and go? And greater will is there to that?

records.m_Talk_ENG: Then we realized. If you try to find out why grass grows or the wind blows, there is an answer in very simple knowledge, not miracles or grace.

records.m_Talk_ENG: But that would imply that death is also placed... where we might find answers.
